descriptions.text: A very big wall built across a river. It holds back the water so a long lake forms behind it. When some water is let through, it falls a long way down and turns big wheels, and the turning wheels make power for the homes and lights of a city.

A tall box in your home that keeps food cold so it stays good to eat for many days. It works by moving heat from the inside of the box to the outside. There is a small light inside that turns on when you open the door.

Stairs that move by themselves. You stand still on one step and it carries you up or down to the next floor, so you do not have to walk at all. At the top, the steps turn flat and slide back down under the floor to start over again.

A set of soft little roads inside your body. A strong working part in the middle of you pushes red life water through these roads all day and all night, without ever stopping, to bring air and food to every small piece of you and to carry the bad stuff away.

Many computers all over the world joined together so they can talk to each other. When you ask for a page, small pieces of it travel through lines under the ground and through the air from far away, and they are put back together in front of you in less than a second.

A very big building where they break the smallest pieces of stuff in the world to make heat. The heat turns water into hot air that pushes big wheels around very fast, and the turning wheels make the lights come on in many far away homes. The people who run it must be very careful, because the stuff inside can make people very sick if it gets out.

A high hill with a deep hole at the top that goes far down into the ground. Sometimes very hot soft rock from deep under the ground comes up through the hole with fire and dark air, and runs down the sides. New ground is made where it cools.

A small box in your home that makes food hot very fast. It sends waves you can not see into the food. The waves make the little bits of water inside the food move very fast, and all that moving is what makes the food hot. It often has a table inside that turns.

The way green living things make their own food. They take light from the sun, water from the ground, and part of the air, and turn them into food so they can grow. While they do this, they give off the very part of the air that we need to stay alive.

A long boat with a hard skin that can go all the way under the water and stay down there for many days. The people inside breathe air that the boat makes for them. It can hide deep in the dark water where no one can see it.

A doctor puts a tiny bit of a very weak or dead form of a bad thing into your arm. Your body learns how to fight it without getting very sick. Later, if the real bad thing tries to get in, your body remembers it and wins the fight fast.

A small flat hard thing you carry with you that lets you buy things now and pay for them later. When you use it, the money house pays for you right away. At the end of the month you must pay the money house back, and if you are late, you will owe them even more.

A little room that moves up and down inside a tall building. You step in, press the number of the floor you want, and it carries you there, pulled by strong lines from above, so you never have to walk up all those stairs.

A very big bag full of hot air, with a small open box hanging under it for people to stand in. A fire makes the air in the bag hot, and because hot air always wants to go up, the whole thing rises off the ground and rides the wind across the sky.

A very bright line of light that jumps between a dark sky and the ground when the weather is bad. It is much hotter than the sun for a tiny moment, and a great loud sound comes after it. It likes to hit high things that stand alone.

A box in your home that cleans your clothes for you. You put the clothes in with water and cleaning stuff, close the door, and it turns them around and around until the dirt comes out. At the end it spins very fast so they come out only a little wet.

You type a few words about what you want to know, and in less than a second it hands you a long list of pages from all over the world that talk about it, with the best ones first. It has already read almost everything, so you do not have to.

A very strong little box that rides in the tail of a flying machine. It remembers everything the machine does and every word said up front. It is built to live through fire and water and a very hard hit, so that if something goes wrong, people can find it and learn what happened.
It looks like a quiet high hill, but it is really a door to the deep hot inside of the world. When it wakes up, it throws hot rock and fire high into the sky, and everyone who lives near it must run.
Without this box, your food would turn bad in a day or two. It is the coldest place in your home, and people often stand in front of it with the door open, looking inside for something good to eat.

It is not in any one place, but it is everywhere. It lets you see faces from the other side of the world, read almost any book, watch anything, buy anything, and say anything, without ever leaving your chair.

Before this was made safe, buildings could not be very tall, because no one wants to walk up forty floors. It is a little room on strong lines that goes up and down all day, and people stand in it quietly, watching the numbers change.

A place with tall round parts that let out white clouds of warm wet air. Deep inside, the smallest bits of a heavy stuff are split, and the huge heat that comes out is turned into the force that lights up a whole city.

People build a great thick wall right where a river runs through a narrow gap in the hills. The water backs up into a huge still body behind it, and as it is let out down below, the push of its fall is turned into power.

Think of your body as a land full of tiny red streams. In the center sits a strong part that never rests, sending the red stuff out to feed every far piece of you and then pulling it all the way back to start again.

A cold box that stands softly in the corner of the room where food is kept. By pulling the heat out of the air inside, it keeps your milk and greens good for far longer than they would last on a warm shelf.

Almost every computer on earth is tied together into one huge thing. Through it, a note or a picture can jump from one side of the world to the other in less time than it takes to shut your eyes, using lines and air.

For years it may sleep like a normal hill. Then one day the ground shakes, and from the top comes hot melted rock, dark dust, and fire, running down the sides in bright lines that burn all they touch.
A little box that heats your food in under a minute. It fills the food with waves you can not see, which shake the water in it so fast that the whole plate turns hot while the box itself stays cool.

A set of steps that carry you along on their own. You just step on and stand, and a hidden part below pulls the whole line of steps up to the floor above, then folds them flat and takes them back down.

Green leaves are like tiny kitchens run on light. They drink up water from below and one part of the air from above, and with the help of the sun they cook it all into food, letting out clean air for us to breathe.

A ship built to live below the waves. It fills parts of itself with water to go down and pushes the water back out to come up, and the people shut inside can stay down in the dark for weeks with the air it makes.

A clever way to teach your body to fight before it ever gets sick. A doctor gives you a tiny taste of a weak dead thing, so that if the real one shows up later, your body already knows just how to win.

A thin flat slip of hard stuff in your bag that stands in for money. You wave or slide it to pay, and a big money house covers the cost for now, trusting that you will pay it all back when the month is done.
A small moving room that saves your legs in a tall building. You walk in, press a number, and strong lines pull you up through the middle of the building to the floor you want.

A great cloth ball that carries people up into the open air. A hot fire heats the air held in the ball, and since warm air likes to climb, the whole thing lifts off the ground and rides wherever the wind blows.

On a dark and wild night, the sky and the ground build up too much of the same push, until it all lets go at once in a blinding white line. The air it tears through gets so hot it makes a great crack of sound.

A round door on a box in the corner where your dirty clothes go in. It spins them round and round in warm water and soap to lift the dirt away, then turns very fast to throw most of the water back out.

A helper that has read nearly every page in the world so you do not have to. You give it a word or two about what you are after, and in a blink it hands back a long, sorted list of the best places to look.

A tough little case that sits in the back of every big flying machine. It quietly writes down what the machine does and what the people up front say, so that after a bad day, others can read it and learn.
It is like a class for the inside of your body. A doctor puts in a small, safe copy of a bad thing, and your body studies it and learns to beat it, so the real thing can never make you very sick.

This is how green leaves make food out of almost nothing. Using the light of the sun as their power, they take in water and one part of the air, and turn it into the sweet stuff they need to grow taller and stronger.
When the sun comes out just after the rain, a big band of many colors bends across the sky, high and wide. Each color sits in its own line, and it slowly goes away as the sky turns clear again.
Look up after a wet day and you might see a soft band of many colors bending high over the world. You can never reach the end of it, no matter how far you walk, for it moves away as fast as you go.
It has two round wheels, one behind the other, and you sit up on top and push it along with your feet. Hold the bar to point it where you want, and lean a little as you turn so you do not fall over.
A way to get around town under your own leg power. You sit on the seat, put your feet on the two arms that go round, and push. The harder you push, the faster the two wheels roll you along the road.
A long flat path built up high to carry you and the cars over a river or a deep drop, so you can get to the other side without going all the way down and then all the way back up again.
When a wide body of water lies in the way, people build one of these across it. It stands on tall legs down in the water and holds up a road, so you can walk or drive right over the top of the water.
A round roof of cloth on a stick that you hold up over your head when the sky is falling wet. It keeps you dry as you walk. When the rain stops, you fold it back up small and put it away.
On a wet day you open this up with a soft push and hold it high. The wet drops hit the top and run off the sides, so your hair and clothes stay dry while you go from place to place.
A flat, smooth piece of special glass that shows you a copy of your own face. When you stand in front of it and lift one hand, the you inside lifts the very same hand right back at you.

People hang this smooth, bright thing on the wall to see how they look. Light hits it and comes right back, so you see your own eyes, your hair, and the whole room behind you, all turned the other way.
A little thing you hold up to the side of your head so you can talk to a person who is far away, and hear them talk right back, as if they were standing next to you in the very same room.

Long ago you had to walk a long way just to say a word to a friend. Now you press some numbers on this, hold it to your ear, and your voice flies through the air to a friend on the far side of the world.
A small box you hold up and point at a thing you want to keep. You press a button, it makes a soft click, and it saves a still, flat copy of just what you saw so you can look at it later.
This is how people keep a moment forever. Stand your friends in a row, point the little round glass eye at them, tell them to smile, and press. Now that happy moment is saved and will never grow old.
At a busy street corner there stands a tall pole with three round eyes on it. The top one glows red to say stop, the middle one says get ready, and the low one glows green to say now you may go.

Cars would run into each other all day without this. It hangs over the road and shows one color at a time to the cars below: red means you wait, and green means it is now your turn to drive on through.
A small closed box full of stored power that you drop into a toy, a clock, or a light to wake it up. After a long while the power runs out, and you must put in a new one or fill this one up again.
You can not see power, but you can keep some inside one of these for later. Toys, phones, and cars all carry them. They hold the push that makes things go, until they run dry and need more.
A very tall pole standing alone in an open field, with three long thin arms up at the top. When the wind blows, the arms go round and round, and that turning is made into power for the homes far off.
Out where the wind blows hard, you see rows of these tall white poles with slow turning arms. They take the free push of the moving air and make it into power, with no smoke and no fire at all.

A long tube with a piece of special glass at each end. You put one end to your eye and point the other at the dark night sky, and things that are very far away, like the moon and the stars, jump up close.

Stars are so far away that they look like tiny points of light. Look at them through this long tube, though, and they grow big and clear. People use it to study the moon and the far lights of the sky.

A small flying bug with soft black and yellow bands on its body. It goes from flower to flower all day, drinking their sweet water and helping new flowers to grow. Make it mad and it can give you a sharp poke.

This little thing buzzes as it flies about the garden. It lives with many of its own kind in a busy home they build together, and it turns the sweet water of flowers into a thick sweet food that we love to eat.

A huge building by the water where they split the tiniest bits of a heavy stuff to make a great deal of heat. That heat boils water into pushing air, and the air spins wheels that light up whole cities far away.
A tall, thick wall set across a river in a deep valley. It holds back so much water that a long lake grows behind it. The water let out at the bottom falls hard and spins big wheels to make power.

Where a river runs through a narrow gap, people wall it off with a great bank of stone. A wide lake grows behind, and the water set free at the base falls hard onto big wheels and spins them to make power.

A great web of soft red tubes that reach every tiny part of you. A strong pump in your chest sends warm red stuff pushing through them, all day and all night, to feed you and carry away what you do not need.
The coldest box in the house, standing tall in the kitchen. It pulls the heat out of the air inside so your food stays good to eat for many more days than it would if you left it out on the table.
Open this cold tall box and a small light clicks on, showing all the food kept cool inside. It works by moving the heat from in the box to out the back, so what sits within stays good far longer.

A huge web you can not see that ties nearly every computer in the world into one. It lets you send a note, see a face, or read a book from far away in the time it takes to blink, all through lines and air.
It is everywhere and nowhere. Through it you can talk to a friend on the far side of the world, watch a show, read a book, or buy a thing, all without ever getting up out of your own chair.
A tall hill with a deep hole down its middle that reaches the hot heart of the world. Now and then it wakes with a great roar and throws hot melted rock, dark dust, and fire high up into the sky.

It looks like a quiet hill for years, but deep down it is a door to the hot inside of the world. When it can hold no more, it blows its top, and burning rock runs down the sides while dust fills the air.
A small box on the kitchen shelf that warms your food fast. It sends waves you can not see into the food, which shake the tiny bits of water inside so hard that the whole plate turns hot in a minute.
In just one minute this little box can turn a cold plate of food hot. It does not use fire at all; it fills the food with waves you can not see that make the water deep inside shake and grow warm.
A set of steps that move by themselves. You stand on one and it carries you up to the floor above while you do nothing at all. At the top each step turns flat and slides back down under the floor.

You step onto the bottom stair, and without lifting a foot you are carried smoothly up to the next floor. It is a line of steps on a moving belt that goes round and round, up the front and back down inside.

The clever way green leaves make their own food. They drink up water from the ground and take one part of the air, and with the help of the light of the sun they turn it all into sweet food, and give off clean air.

A long ship with a hard skin, built to sink down deep and live below the waves for weeks at a time. It carries its own air for the people shut inside, and it can hide in the dark water where none can see.

This ship does its work under the sea, not on top of it. It lets water in to sink and blows the water back out to rise, and deep in the dark it can go for weeks while the people inside breathe air it makes.

A doctor gives your arm a tiny poke that carries a weak, dead bit of a bad thing. Your body sees it and learns how to fight it, so if the real thing ever comes, your body wins the fight fast and you stay well.

A thin flat slip of hard stuff you keep in your bag to buy things with. When you slide it, a big money house pays for you right now, and you pay the house back at the end of the month, or you owe even more.

A little room that slides up and down inside a tall building on strong ropes. Step in, press the number of the floor you want, and it carries you there in a few moments so you never have to climb the stairs.

A great round bag of hot air with a little basket hung below for people to stand in. A flame heats the air in the bag, and since warm air likes to go up, the whole thing rises slowly and floats across the sky.
On a dark and wild night, the sky and the ground fill up with too much of the same push. All at once it lets go in a bright, sharp white line, and a great crack of sound rolls out right after it.

This is the bright, sudden line of fire that jumps between the clouds and the ground when the weather turns wild. For a tiny moment it is hotter than the sun, and the loud crack it makes can shake the windows.

A box in the corner with a round door where your dirty clothes go. It turns them over and over in warm water and soap to pull the dirt out, then spins so fast that the clothes come out only a little wet.

It has read nearly every page in the whole world, so you do not have to. You type in a word or two about what you want to know, and in less than a blink it hands you a long, sorted list of the best places to look.

A small, very tough case that rides in the tail of every big flying machine. It writes down all the machine does and every word the people up front say, so if things go wrong, others can find it and learn what went bad.
